data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- Commit your work yourself as you finish coherent pieces — `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>` (see the horizon-commit skill). Small semantic commits (a lemma closed, a file completed) make the history and the dashboard's change view legible; don't leave one undifferentiated blob for the end-of-session sweep. Other runs may commit concurrently to the same ledger — that's fine; commit only the files you changed.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0008/sessions/0007-horizon-T5

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
# Recommendation — run 0008 reconcile Ground (T5, `AJC.picrep`/`AJC.pic0av` spine)

Suggestions for the next Horizon/Ground agent. Grounded in the T5 reconcile; you self-scope.

## Next

- **`tangentSpaceIso` (`AJC.pic0av`) is now the single highest-value target** — the FGA rewire removed the opaque-`picSharp` blocker that made it unattackable. The move: attach **Kleiman Thm 5.11** (`Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + the truncated-exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1`, kernel `= H¹(C, O_C)`) through `(representable C).homEquiv` at `T = Spec k[ε]`. None of this H¹ machinery exists yet — it is the **largest remaining block** and now genuinely unblocked.

- **`Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety`'s `GrpObj` conjunct is now provable** via `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` (`groupSchemeStructure` supplies `GrpObj (PicScheme C)`). Worth **splitting the 4-way conjunction** (proper ∧ smooth ∧ GrpObj ∧ finrank=genus) so the provable parts close now instead of waiting on the whole.

- **The FGA rewire is conditional on `[HasRationalPoint C]`** (see `I-0077`). Before anyone assembles the north star `AJC.jacobian`, decide: Galois/étale descent from a pointed finite extension, **or** a pointed restatement of `JacobianWitness`. This is a strategy call, not proof-search.

## Watch-outs

- **Build hazard is live**: a concurrent `claude-fable-5` Horizon agent + one `lean` compiler process were running during this reconcile. The FGA/RelPic/pic0av cone is **FBC-free** (does not import the `Cech*` chain), so it is safe to iterate there — but still `pgrep -f bin/lean` before any `lake build`, and never touch the `Cech*` oleans.

- `SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent` mirror is **further behind AJC** now (missing the rewired `FGAPicRepresentability`/`IdentityComponent` + `Pic0Scheme` real def). Re-sync or retire; out of this session's write scope.

- The genuine FGA `sorry` `instHasPicScheme` is the real Grothendieck existence theorem (Kleiman §4 `th:main` + `cor:algsch`) — a large build, not a quick win. Do not re-probe the `HasDivFunctor`/`HasAbelMap` carriers either; they are Quot-scheme-blocked (χ/Hilbert-polynomial endgame).

# Recommended focus
Task: T5
Title: Extract & prove Pic⁰ IdentityComponent (FBC-independent picrep spine)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent*.lean

Advance the Picard-representability (picrep) spine WITHOUT depending on flat base change (FBC/02KH), so this runs fully in parallel with the live FBC run (T2) in AJC.

STEP 0 (gate — do this first, report before proceeding): Verify that the `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` import cone is genuinely FBC/cohomology-independent. Trace its transitive imports in MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge. If the cone pulls in the Čech/cohomology base-change engine (Cohomology/Cech*, the 02KH flat-base-change modules), STOP and report — do not extract; instead reduce scope to only the FBC-free declarations. If clean, continue.

STEP 1 (extraction — own build dir, MUST NOT build the AJC tree): Create a NEW standalone Lean subproject (e.g. SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent) with its own lakefile.toml (require mathlib rev v4.31.0, packagesDir ../../.lake-packages), lean-toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.31.0, and its own .lake — mirroring the Albanese/GR-Quot-Closure extraction pattern. Copy in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` and the minimal import cone needed to build it. Register it with `horizon project add` (enable vcs git_dir). The point of a separate subproject is build isolation: it must NOT touch or rebuild the AJC .lake that T2 is using.

STEP 2 (prove): Close the 9 structural sorries in IdentityComponent — Pic⁰ as the open-and-closed connected-component subgroup functor and the abelian-group-structure (Yoneda) transport — the parts that do NOT need the cohomology / flat-base-change engine. Leave any sorry that genuinely depends on FBC or full FGA representability documented and untouched, with a note on the blocker.

DELIVERABLE: the new subproject builds GREEN (lake build EXIT=0) with the FBC-free IdentityComponent sorries closed and axiom-clean; blueprint/DAG updated; it is positioned to merge back into AJC sorry-free like Albanese/GR. Reads from the AJC tree as source.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **Beck–Chevalley heart of 02KG closed** (T2): `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC` and the affine essential-image member node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`) are sorry-free and axiom-clean; full AJC build green. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`: the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields + the per-$\sigma$ RHS tilde leaf, and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact) — 5 leaf sorries. See task **T2**.
- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.

For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **Stacks 01BG general pullback-qcoh CLOSED + FBC RHS tilde leaf CLOSED (run 0003 session 0013, T2) — supersedes all "general-morphism pullback qcoh is absent / only in sorry-tainted QuotScheme" notes.**

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.
- Enablers now available: `opensMap_final` (`(Opens.map φ).Final` for ANY continuous map — structured-arrow posets are ⊔-directed, `Functor.final_of_isFiltered_structuredArrow`) and `pullbackUnitIso : g^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y` (Mathlib's `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso under `[F.Final]`). Gotcha: re-synthesis of `IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit …)` at USE sites can fail (defeq wall in the instance-arg unification) — pass the instance explicitly via `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_hom g)`.
- FBC leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` (622-line sorry) closed as literally `(pushforward f').mapIso (twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma …) ≪≫ pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde f' 𝒰' (g'^*F) (pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hF) σ` — the def had to MOVE below `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` in the file. FBC leaves 5 → 4.
- Iteration cost: `lake env lean` on a file importing Mathlib+`CechTermAcyclic` ≈ 4 min under load ~65; the 2-module `lake build` (new file + `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`) ≈ 25 min.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.

28	projectivity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` for `C/k` geometrically normal, and the assembly

32

41	   canonical isomorphism `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` (Kleiman §5
42	   Thm.~`thm:tgtsp`). Packaged as an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space

139	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]

144	      (e ≫ (Pic0Scheme C).hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) ×'
145	      ({g : Spec (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left //
146	          g ≫ (Pic0Scheme C).hom
147	              = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k (DualNumber k)))
148	            ∧ g.base (IsLocalRing.closedPoint (DualNumber k)) = e.base default} ≃
149	        Module.Dual
150	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default)))
151	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace
152	            ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))))) := by
153	  obtain ⟨i⟩ := hgrp
154	  letI := i
155	  haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
156	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))
157	  exact ⟨GroupScheme.identitySection (Pic0Scheme C),
158	    GroupScheme.identitySection_comp (Pic0Scheme C),
159	    overDualNumberSectionEquivCotangentSpaceDual (Pic0Scheme C)
160	      (GroupScheme.identitySection_comp (Pic0Scheme C))
161	      (congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _))⟩
162	
163	/-- **Tangent space at the identity: `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.**
164	
165	The Kleiman §5 Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` tangent-space isomorphism. For a smooth
166	proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the Zariski tangent space at the
167	identity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is canonically isomorphic to the first sheaf
168	cohomology `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
169	
170	In Lean (skeleton form): we bundle existentially over a `k`-rational
171	identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
172	structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the natural `k`-module structure
173	arising from the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
174	for the file-skeleton this instance is supplied via Σ' to avoid hard-coding
175	a global instance prematurely). The substantive content is an `AddEquiv`
176	between the cotangent space and the project's `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` module
177	`Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`.

179	A k-LinearEquiv refinement (iter-194+) replaces the `AddEquiv` once the
180	`k`-module structure on the cotangent space is consistently threaded through
181	downstream consumers; the underlying additive group structure is enough for
182	the dimension corollary `dim_k T₀ Pic⁰ = dim_k H¹ = g(C)`. -/
183	theorem tangentSpaceIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
184	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
185	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
186	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C]
187	    [PicScheme.PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :
188	    Nonempty (Σ' (e : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left),
189	      IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))
190	        ≃+ Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
191	  sorry
192	
193	/-- **Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
194	
195	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
196	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is smooth over `k` (Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` in
197	characteristic zero + Ex.~`ex:jac` for the curve case).
198	
199	The proof (iter-194+): smoothness at the identity propagates to smoothness
200	everywhere by translation (Pic⁰ is a `k`-group scheme, so multiplication by
201	any `λ ∈ Pic⁰(k̄)` is an automorphism). At the identity, smoothness reduces
202	to the inequality
203	`dim_0 Pic⁰_{C/k} ≤ dim_k T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} = dim_k H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = g(C)`
204	(`tangentSpaceIso`) becoming an equality. For a smooth proper curve this
205	equality holds: in characteristic zero by Cartier's theorem (every
206	`k`-group scheme is smooth), in positive characteristic by the curve-specific
207	vanishing `H²(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the obstruction to lifting tangent vectors). -/

1001	/-! ## §3. The degree map
1002	
1003	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
1004	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
1005	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
1006	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
1007	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
1008	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
1009	
1010	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
1011	
1012	namespace PicScheme
1013	
1014	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
1015	
1016	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
1017	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
1018	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
1019	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
1020	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
1021	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
1022	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
1023	relative Picard functor).
1024	
1025	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
1026	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
1027	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
1028	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
1029	
1030	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
1031	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
1032	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
1033	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
1034	`sorry`. -/

1037	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
1038	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
1039	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
1040	  sorry

1042	end PicScheme

1044	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
1045	
1046	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
1047	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
1048	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
1049	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
1050	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
1051	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
1052	
1053	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
1054	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
1055	
1056	namespace Pic0Scheme
1057	
1058	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
1059	
1060	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
1061	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
1062	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
1063	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
1064	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
1065	
1066	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
1067	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
1068	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
1069	
1070	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
1071	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
1072	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
1073	follow-up.

1076	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
1077	  `PicScheme C`),
1078	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
1079	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
1080	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
1081	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
1082	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
1083	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
1084	  identity component).

1090	    [PicScheme.PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :
1091	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
1092	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
1093	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
1094	  sorry
1095	
1096	/-- **Dimension of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals the genus of `C`.**
1097	
1098	Milne~§III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e): "The dimension of `J` is the genus of `C`".
1099	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k` of genus
1100	`g = g(C)`, the topological Krull dimension of the underlying scheme of
1101	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` equals `g(C)`. By Kleiman~§5 Cor.~`cor:sm`, the inequality
1102	`dim Pic_{C/k} ≤ dim_k H¹(C, O_C)` is always an equality at points where
1103	`Pic_{C/k}` is smooth, and for smooth proper curves
1104	(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom`) the identity component is smooth
1105	(Kleiman~§5 Ex.~`ex:jac`), so the dimension equals `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)`
1106	by `def:genus`. -/
1107	theorem finrank_eq_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
1108	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
1109	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
1110	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C]
1111	    [PicScheme.PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :
1112	    topologicalKrullDim (Pic0Scheme C).left = (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
1113	  sorry
1114	
1115	/-- **`k`-points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` are the kernel of the degree map.**
1116	
1117	Milne~§III.1, p.~88: `Pic⁰(C)` is the group of isomorphism classes of
1118	invertible sheaves of degree zero on `C`. For a smooth proper geometrically
1119	integral curve `C/k`, a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` lies in the image of
1120	the inclusion `Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}` (the inclusion of
1121	`def:pic_zero_subscheme`, packaged here via the existence of the
1122	inclusion morphism) if and only if `degree C λ = 0`.
1123

1125	morphism `Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶ Pic_{C/k}` (extracted from
1126	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` once `PicScheme C` has the
1127	`GrpObj` + `LocallyOfFiniteType` instances), together with the
1128	characterisation of `k`-points factoring through it as those with degree

1131	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))

1133	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C]
1134	    [PicScheme.PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :

1136	      ∀ (lambda : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left),
1137	        (∃ mu : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left, mu ≫ inc = lambda) ↔
1138	          PicScheme.degree C lambda = 0) :=
1139	  sorry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Presheaf
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.SheafProperty
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Carriers

/-!
# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf (re-export)

Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`) is split across three sub-files:

* `StructureSheafModuleK/Presheaf.lean` — categorical gap-fills (the
  `CategoryTheory.Functor.const_*` / `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.*` instances),
  the `HasSheafify` / `HasExt` typeclass prerequisites, the per-open ring-map
  helpers (1)–(5), and the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6).
* `StructureSheafModuleK/SheafProperty.lean` — the sheaf condition
  `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), the bundled `toModuleKSheaf` (8), and the
  forget-and-recover natural iso to the `AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf.
* `StructureSheafModuleK/Carriers.lean` — the `HModule` / `HModule'` abbrevs,
  the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` and
  `IsHModuleHomFinite` carriers, the Stein-finiteness input, the constant-sheaf-Γ
  adjunction upgrade, the `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf` producer
  instance, and the Čech-side carriers `Scheme.cechCochain[_OC]`,
  `Scheme.cechCohomology[_OC]`, `Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover`.

See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
-/

103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=

\begin{theorem}[Dual-number points of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) at the identity]\leanok
  \label{thm:pic0_tangent_cotangent_dual}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceCotangentDual}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_open_subgroup}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
  field \(k\), and suppose \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) carries its \(k\)-group-scheme
  structure (\cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup}). Then there
  is an identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec} k \to
  \Pic^0_{C/k}\) of the structure morphism such that the dual-number
  points of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) at \(e(*)\) over \(\operatorname{Spec} k\)
  form the \(\kappa(e(*))\)-linear dual of the cotangent space:
  \[
    \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k}
    \;=\;
    \{\, g : \operatorname{Spec} k_\varepsilon \to \Pic^0_{C/k}
        \text{ over } \operatorname{Spec} k
        \mid g(\mathfrak m) = e(*) \,\}
    \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{Hom}_{\kappa(e(*))}\!\bigl(
      \mathfrak m_{e(*)}/\mathfrak m_{e(*)}^2,\, \kappa(e(*))\bigr).
  \]
  This is the geometric half of \cref{thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso};
  what remains is the identification of the right-hand side with
  \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}\leanok
  \uses{thm:tangent_identity_cotangent_dual}
  The group-scheme structure provides the identity section, and
  \cref{thm:tangent_identity_cotangent_dual} applied to
  \(G = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) gives the identification.
\end{proof}


\begin{theorem}\leanok
[Tangent space at the identity: \(\tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} \cong H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)]
  \label{thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_open_subgroup,
        def:genus}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Thm.~thm:tgtsp;
  % arXiv:math/0504020, p.~41 (read from
  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L3265-L3270).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{thm}\label{thm:tgtsp}
  %  Assume \(S\) is the spectrum of a field \(k\).  Assume \(\IPic_{X/k}\) exists
  % and represents \(\Pic_{(X/k)\et}\).  Let \(\tu T_0\IPic_{X/k}\) denote the
  % tangent space at \(0\).  Then
  %    \(\)\tu T_0\IPic_{X/k}=\tu H^1(\mc O_{\!X}).\(\)
  %  \end{thm}"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5,
  Thm.~5.11; cf.\ [Hartshorne], ``Algebraic Geometry'', GTM 52,
  IV.1.1 (genus def.\ \(g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)).}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus
  \(g = g(C)\) over a field \(k\), and let \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) be the identity component of the
  Picard scheme of \(C/k\). There is a canonical isomorphism of
  \(k\)-vector spaces
  \[
    \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} \;\cong\; H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C).
  \]
  In particular, the \(k\)-dimension of \(\tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k}\) equals
  the genus \(g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)
  (\cref{def:genus}).
\end{theorem}

data.content:
\label{sec:pic0_geom_irred}

Geometric irreducibility is delivered directly by the abstract
identity-component substrate
(\cref{thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible}) applied
to the \(k\)-group scheme \(G = \Pic_{C/k}\). We restate the
specialisation here for the assembly downstream.

\begin{theorem}\leanok
[Geometric irreducibility of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)]
  \label{thm:pic0_geom_irred}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Prp.~prp:pic0;
  % arXiv:math/0504020, p.~36 (read from
  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L2921-L2929).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{prp}\label{prp:pic0}
  %   Assume \(S\) is the spectrum of a field \(k\).  Assume \(\IPic_{X/k}\)
  % exists and represents \(\Pic_{(X/k)\fppf}\).  Then \(\IPic_{X/k}\) is
  % separated, and it is smooth if it has a geometrically reduced open
  % subscheme.  Furthermore, the connected component of the identity
  % \(\IPicz_{X/k}\) is an open and closed group subscheme of finite type; it
  % is geometrically irreducible; and forming it commutes with extending
  % \(k\).
  %  \end{prp}"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Prp.~5.3
  (geometric irreducibility of the connected component of the
  identity).}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
  field \(k\). The identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) is geometrically irreducible: after
  base change to the algebraic closure \(\bar k\), the scheme
  \((\Pic^0_{C/k})_{\bar k}\) is irreducible.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible,
        thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes,
        thm:fga_pic_representability}
  Apply the abstract identity-component substrate to the \(k\)-group
  scheme \(G = \Pic_{C/k}\), which is locally of finite type by
  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}. By
  \cref{thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible}, the
  identity component \((\Pic_{C/k})^0 = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) is of finite
  type and geometrically irreducible over \(k\). The base-change
  compatibility of
  \cref{thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes} identifies
  \((\Pic^0_{C/k})_{\bar k}\) with \(\Pic^0_{C_{\bar k}/\bar k}\),
  closing the proof.
\end{proof}

\section{Assembly: \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an abelian variety}
\label{sec:pic0_isAbelianVariety}

A \(k\)-group scheme is an \emph{abelian variety} iff its underlying
\(k\)-scheme is a complete (proper) connected group variety
(Milne~\S I.1, p.~8). Combining the smoothness, properness, and
geometric irreducibility statements of this chapter with the
identity-component group-scheme structure of
\cref{chap:Picard_IdentityComponent} packages \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) as an
abelian variety of dimension \(g\). This is the load-bearing A.3.vii
gate for Route~A.

\begin{theorem}\leanok
[\(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an abelian variety]
  \label{thm:pic0_isAbelianVariety}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.isAbelianVariety}
  \uses{thm:pic0_smooth,
        thm:pic0_proper,
        thm:pic0_geom_irred,
        thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism}

  % SOURCE: [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'' (course notes, 2008),
  % \S I.1, p.~8 (definition of abelian variety) (read from
  % references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF p.~14).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "A complete connected group variety is called an abelian variety. As we
  % shall see, they are projective, and (fortunately) commutative. Their
  % group laws will be written additively. Thus \(t_a\) is now denoted
  % \(x \mapsto x + a\) and \(e\) is usually denoted \(0\)."
  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Rmk.~rmk:Jac;
  % arXiv:math/0504020, p.~51 (read from
  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L3990-L3996).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{rmk}\label{rmk:Jac}
  %  Assume \(X/S\) is projective and smooth, its geometric fibers are
  % connected curves of genus \(g>0\), and \(S\) is Noetherian.  Set
  % \(J:=\IPicz_{X/S}\); it exists and is a projective Abelian \(S\)-scheme by
  % Exercise~\ref{ex:jac}."
  \textit{Source: [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'' (course notes, 2008),
  \S I.1, p.~8 (definition of abelian variety); [Kleiman], ``The
  Picard scheme'', \S 5, Rmk.~5.26 (the Jacobian
  \(J := \Pic^0_{X/S}\) of a smooth proper family of curves is a
  projective abelian \(S\)-scheme).}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
  genus \(g\) over a field \(k\). The identity component
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) is an
  \emph{abelian variety over \(k\)} of dimension \(g\): the underlying
  \(k\)-scheme is smooth (\cref{thm:pic0_smooth}), proper
  (\cref{thm:pic0_proper}), and geometrically irreducible
  (\cref{thm:pic0_geom_irred}), and it carries a \(k\)-group-scheme
  structure inherited from \(\Pic_{C/k}\) via the inclusion
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \hookrightarrow \Pic_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism}). In
  particular, \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is automatically commutative (Milne~\S
  I.1, Cor.~1.4).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:pic0_smooth,
        thm:pic0_proper,
        thm:pic0_geom_irred,
        thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism,
        thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible}
  We assemble Milne's defining axioms of an abelian variety from the
  four conjuncts established earlier in this chapter and in
  \cref{chap:Picard_IdentityComponent}.

  \emph{(i) Group-variety structure.} By
  \cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism} applied to
  \(G = \Pic_{C/k}\) (a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type by
  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}), the identity component
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) inherits a \(k\)-group-scheme structure from
  \(\Pic_{C/k}\) compatible with the clopen inclusion.

  \emph{(ii) Smoothness.} By \cref{thm:pic0_smooth}, the structural
  morphism \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Spec k\) is smooth (of relative
  dimension \(g\)). In particular \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is a smooth
  \(k\)-variety: it is reduced (Milne's standing convention bundles
  reducedness into ``variety''), geometrically reduced (by
  smoothness over \(k\)), and locally of finite type.

  \emph{(iii) Properness.} By \cref{thm:pic0_proper}, the structural
  morphism \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Spec k\) is proper (in fact
  projective). This is Milne's ``complete'' axiom.

  \emph{(iv) Connectedness (geometric irreducibility).} By
  \cref{thm:pic0_geom_irred}, the underlying scheme
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is geometrically irreducible; in particular it is
  geometrically connected, hence connected.

  Combining (i)--(iv): \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is a smooth, proper,
  geometrically irreducible \(k\)-scheme equipped with a
  \(k\)-group-scheme structure. By Milne~\S I.1, p.~8, ``a complete
  connected group variety is called an abelian variety''. Thus
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an abelian variety over \(k\); commutativity is
  automatic (Milne~\S I.1, Cor.~1.4). Its dimension is the relative
  dimension of the smooth morphism \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Spec k\),
  which equals \(g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\) by
  \cref{thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso} and \cref{thm:pic0_smooth}.

  This is the content of Kleiman~\S 5 Rmk.~5.26: for \(X/S\)
  projective and smooth with connected curve fibres of genus
  \(g > 0\) (specialise to \(S = \Spec k\), \(X = C\)), the Jacobian
  \(J := \Pic^0_{X/S}\) exists and is a projective abelian
  \(S\)-scheme. The genus-zero case is degenerate
  (\(\Pic^0_{C/k} = 0\), Milne~\S III.1, Rmk.~1.4(e)) but is
  consistent with the assembly: a point scheme \(\Spec k\) is
  trivially an abelian variety of dimension \(0\).
\end{proof}

\section{Lean encoding}
\label{sec:pic0_isAbelianVariety_lean_encoding}

input.description: Read PicScheme, HasPicScheme, groupSchemeStructure, LFT instances

data.content:
disjoint union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the
abelian-group-scheme structure is `groupSchemeStructure` below.

Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/

/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
the relative Picard functor `picSharp C`. The single sorry-carrying site for
`PicScheme` is the `⟨sorry⟩` instance below, **conditional on
`[HasRationalPoint C]`** — without a section the plain relative functor is
not representable in general, so an unconditional instance would assert a
false statement. Consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/
class HasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)

/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C/k`, encoded as an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.

Extracted (`Classical.choose`) from the `HasPicScheme` existence field, so it
carries the substantive identity "this scheme represents the relative Picard
functor `picSharp C`" through `representable` below — which, after the
run-0008 rewire, is a genuine natural bijection onto
`Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)`, not an opaque placeholder. -/
noncomputable def PicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
  (HasPicScheme.has_pic_scheme (C := C)).choose

/-- Existence instance for `HasPicScheme` — **the single genuine FGA sorry of
this file**. Mathematical content: Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main` + Cor
`cor:algsch` (the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor of a smooth proper
geometrically integral curve is representable by a separated `k`-scheme
locally of finite type), combined with Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5 (under the
`k`-rational point of `[HasRationalPoint C]`, the plain relative functor
`picSharp C` coincides with the étale-sheafified one, so the same scheme
represents it).

The conditionality on `[HasRationalPoint C]` is essential for truth: without
a section, `Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` need not even be a Zariski sheaf. -/
noncomputable instance instHasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasRationalPoint C] : HasPicScheme C :=
  ⟨sorry⟩

namespace PicScheme

/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence

The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` determines a
`T`-point of `Div_{C/k}`, and the dual ideal sheaf
`O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` represents a class in `Pic^♯_{C/k}(T)`.

Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/

/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/k}`.
The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
`abelMap`. (The bare `Nonempty` statement is true — the pointwise-zero
    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ CommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
  PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.toCommGrp

/-- The set-valued shadows of `picSharpCommGrp` and `picSharp` agree up to
the canonical `Multiplicative.ofAdd` bijection (a natural isomorphism, since
`Multiplicative` is carrier-preserving). -/
noncomputable def picSharpCommGrpForgetIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] :
    picSharp C ≅ picSharpCommGrp C ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget CommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
  NatIso.ofComponents
    (fun T => Equiv.toIso (Multiplicative.ofAdd
      (α := ((PicSharp.relPresheaf C).obj T : Type (u+1)))))
    (fun _ => rfl)

/-- **The Picard scheme is a commutative `k`-group scheme** — a canonical
`CommGrpObj` structure (i.e. `GrpObj` + `IsCommMonObj`) on `PicScheme C`,
obtained by Yoneda transport (`CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`) of the
abelian-group structure of the relative Picard presheaf
`PicSharp.relPresheaf` along the representability witness `representable C`.
Concretely: `[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`, `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`, unit `[O_{C ×_k T}]`,
transported through `(T ⟶ Pic_{C/k}) ≃ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)`.

Run-0008: this is now a real construction (the former `⟨sorry⟩`-backed
`PicSchemeGroupObject` carrier class is deleted), and it is STRONGER than
the old pinned statement: commutativity comes for free from the transport,
so the full abelian-group-scheme refinement promised by the blueprint
(`thm:pic_is_group_scheme`) lands in one step. -/
noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] :
    CommGrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
  CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (PicScheme C) (picSharpCommGrp C)
    ((representable C).ofIso (picSharpCommGrpForgetIso C))

/-! ## §6. Local finiteness of the Picard scheme

Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main`(1): the Picard scheme is separated and locally of
finite type over the base. `HasPicScheme` packages only the representability
existential (run-0005 T5 check, memory `I-0037`), so local finiteness needs
its own carrier. It is what the identity-component substrate
(`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`, `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent`) consumes
to specialise to `G = PicScheme C`. -/

/-- Typeclass asserting that the structural morphism of the Picard scheme is
locally of finite type (Kleiman §4 Thm `th:main`(1): `Pic_{C/k}` is a
disjoint union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, hence locally of
finite type). The `⟨sorry⟩` instance below, conditional on
`[HasRationalPoint C]`, is the unique sorry-carrying site; the statement is
true because representing objects are unique up to isomorphism and
`LocallyOfFiniteType` is invariant under isomorphism, so the property
transfers from Kleiman's construction to the `Classical.choose` witness. -/
class PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C] : Prop where
  locallyOfFiniteType : LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom

/-- Existence instance for `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType`, conditional on the
`k`-rational point (like `instHasPicScheme`, on which it depends). This is
one of the sorry-carrying sites of the file; content: Kleiman §4 Thm
`th:main`(1). -/
instance instPicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasRationalPoint C] :
    PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C :=
  ⟨sorry⟩

/-- Projection of `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` to the Mathlib morphism
property, so that instance search finds `LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme
C).hom` whenever the carrier class is in scope (as the identity-component
substrate requires). -/
instance {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasPicScheme C]
    [PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType C] :
    LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom :=
  PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType.locallyOfFiniteType

input.description: Read the IdentityComponent chapter isAbelianVariety node

data.content:
an abelian variety of dimension \(g\) --- the Jacobian variety of \(C\).
Recall (Milne~\S I.1, p.~8) that a \(k\)-group scheme is an
\emph{abelian variety} iff it is a complete (proper) connected group
variety over \(k\); commutativity and projectivity then follow
automatically.

% The three conclusions of the original ``\(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an abelian
% variety of dimension \(g\)'' theorem are split below into per-conclusion
% blocks, each with its own \lean{...} pin. The Kleiman ex:jac + rmk:Jac
% verbatim source quote (covering the abelian-variety structure) is
% reproduced once at \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety}; the Milne
% III.1.4(e) verbatim quote (covering the dimension formula) is at
% \cref{thm:pic_zero_dimension_equals_genus}; the Milne III.1 quote
% characterising \(\Pic^0(C)\) as degree-zero classes is already reproduced
% in \cref{def:divisor_degree_pic} and referenced from
% \cref{thm:pic_zero_k_points_iff_degree_zero}. Each theorem carries its
% own \begin{proof} block: the Kleiman th:qpp\&p source-proof quote
% precedes the proof of \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety}, and the
% Cor.~5.13 source-proof quote precedes the proof of
% \cref{thm:pic_zero_dimension_equals_genus}.

\begin{theorem}
[\(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an abelian variety]
  \label{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme, thm:identity_component_open_subgroup, thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism, thm:identity_component_finite_type_geom_irreducible, thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes, thm:fga_pic_representability, thm:pic_is_group_scheme}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Ex.~5.23 + Rmk.~5.26;
  % arXiv:math/0504020, pp.~50--51 (read from
  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L3911-L3917 +
  % L3990-L3996).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{ex}\label{ex:jac}
  % Assume \(X/S\) is projective and flat, its geometric fibers are integral
  % curves of arithmetic genus \(p_a\), and \(S\) is Noetherian.  Show the
  % ``generalized Jacobians'' \(\IPicz_{X_s/k_s}\) form a smooth
  % quasi-projective family of relative dimension \(p_a\).  And show this
  % family is projective if and only if \(X/S\) is smooth.
  % \end{ex}"
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{rmk}\label{rmk:Jac}
  %  Assume \(X/S\) is projective and smooth, its geometric fibers are
  % connected curves of genus \(g>0\), and \(S\) is Noetherian.  Set
  % \(J:=\IPicz_{X/S}\); it exists and is a projective Abelian \(S\)-scheme by
  % Exercise~\ref{ex:jac}."
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5,
  Ex.~5.23 + Rmk.~5.26; cf.\ [Milne], ``Abelian Varieties'',
  \S I.1 (def.\ of abelian variety, p.~8).}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus
  \(g\) over a field \(k\). The identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) of
  \cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme} is an \emph{abelian variety over
  \(k\)}: it is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible as a
  \(k\)-scheme, and it carries a \(k\)-group-scheme structure. (In the
  Lean encoding this is the conjunction of
  \texttt{[IsProper]}, \texttt{[Smooth]}, and
  \texttt{[GeometricallyIrreducible]} on the structural morphism
  \texttt{(Pic0Scheme C).hom} together with the existence of a
  \texttt{[GrpObj]} structure on \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\). Commutativity
  follows automatically from Milne~\S I.1, Cor.~1.4 and is not
  separately stated.)
\end{theorem}

input.description: Read blueprint conventions skill

data.content:
---
name: blueprint-conventions
description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
---

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.grpObj}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
  field \(k\). The identity component \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) carries a \(k\)-group-scheme
  structure, inherited from the group scheme \(\Pic_{C/k}\) via the
  clopen inclusion \(\Pic^0_{C/k} \hookrightarrow \Pic_{C/k}\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}\leanok

  (\cref{thm:pic_is_group_scheme}) locally of finite type over \(k\)

  definition its identity component. By
  \cref{thm:identity_component_is_subgroup_homomorphism} the identity
  component of a \(k\)-group scheme locally of finite type is a
  subgroup scheme; in particular \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) inherits a
  \(k\)-group-scheme structure compatible with the inclusion.
\end{proof}

input.description: Read rest of the pic_zero_is_abelian_variety proof

data.content:
of finite type over \(k\), geometrically irreducible, and its
  formation commutes with extending \(k\). In particular \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  is separated and locally of finite type as a subscheme of
  \(\Pic_{C/k}\), and connected.

  (ii) \emph{Quasi-projectivity.} The disjoint-union structure of
  \(\Pic_{C/k}\) (\cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}: a disjoint union
  of open quasi-projective \(k\)-subschemes) restricted to \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  expresses the identity component as a quasi-projective \(k\)-scheme
  (Kleiman~\S 5, Thm.~5.4: \(X/k\) projective and geometrically
  integral \(\Longrightarrow\) \(\Pic^0_{X/k}\) is quasi-projective).

  (iii) \emph{Projectivity (properness).} For a smooth proper
  geometrically integral curve \(C/k\) of genus \(g > 0\), \(C/k\) is
  also geometrically normal (the smoothness implies geometric
  regularity, hence geometric normality). Kleiman~\S 5, Thm.~5.4
  then upgrades the quasi-projective conclusion to projective:
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is projective over \(k\), in particular proper.
  The proof of Theorem~5.4 uses the Chevalley--Rosenlicht structure
  theorem to reduce to ruling out non-constant maps
  \(\mathbb G_m \to \Pic^0_{C/k}\), which on a geometrically normal
  \(X\) becomes a divisor-theoretic computation.

  (iv) \emph{Smoothness.} For a smooth proper curve \(C/k\), the
  Picard scheme is smooth at \(0\): this is Exercise~5.23 of
  Kleiman~\S 5, which asserts that for \(X/S\) projective and flat
  with integral geometric fibres of arithmetic genus \(p_a\), the
  generalised Jacobians \(\Pic^0_{X_s/k_s}\) form a smooth
  quasi-projective family of relative dimension \(p_a\); specialising
  to \(S = \Spec k\) and \(X = C\) a smooth proper curve of (geometric
  = arithmetic) genus \(g\) gives smoothness of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\).

  Combining (i)--(iv): \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is a smooth, projective,
  geometrically irreducible \(k\)-group scheme. A proper connected
  \(k\)-group variety is by definition an \emph{abelian variety}
  (Milne~\S I.1, p.~8), and is automatically commutative (Milne~\S I.1,
  Cor.~1.4). This establishes
  \cref{thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety}.
\end{proof}

data.content:
---
name: horizon-commit
description: Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
---

Commit your work yourself, semantically, as you finish coherent pieces — don't
leave one giant undifferentiated snapshot for the system to sweep up. Good
commits are how the dashboard's change view and a human reviewer understand what
each session actually did.

## How

Use the Horizon CLI (never raw `git` on the ledger — the workspace repo is
out-of-tree with excludes, a secret guard, and provenance trailers that a bare
`git` would bypass):

- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" <files…>` — commit the files you changed.
  Paths are relative to your current directory (your project dir) or absolute.
- `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<message>" --changed` — commit everything you
  changed in the project (excludes the shared Horizon state, which the system
  commits separately).

Provenance (run / session / role / task / projects) is stamped automatically —
do **not** pass it. The commit is attributed to your role.

## Messages — math first

Write the message the way you'd tell a mathematician what you did: the result
and the idea, in human-readable prose with LaTeX where it helps; mention Lean
declaration names / files only as supporting detail. Mirror the house style,
e.g. `Prove H^i(X,F)=0 for i>dim X via the Čech-to-derived comparison`. One
coherent step per commit; split unrelated work into separate commits.

## Concurrency — you are not alone

Other runs may be committing to the **same** ledger at the same time. That's
fine and intended (it helps everyone converge faster):

- Commits are a single shared branch — they serialise and never conflict as a
  git *merge*; if `horizon commit` hits contention it already retried, so trust
  its result.
- Commit only the files **you** changed. On a shared worktree your commit can
  still capture another run's in-flight edit to the same file — that's expected
  and acceptable; the message + trailers still record your intent.
- Never `reset`, `rebase`, force-push, or otherwise rewrite the ledger. `horizon
  commit` only ever appends a commit.

## When

Commit at each coherent milestone (a lemma closed, a file completed), not only
at the end. Anything you don't commit is still captured by the system's
end-of-session sweep, but as an unlabelled blob — so commit it yourself to keep
the history legible.
